message:
fix(obligation): deterministic collections, prevent failed close from poisoning state

choreography/mod.rs:
- Replace HashSet with BTreeSet for loop_labels in validate_interaction to
  ensure deterministic iteration order during validation. When multiple loop
  label errors exist, HashSet's random iteration order could cause different
  error orderings across runs, making lab replay comparison unreliable

separation_logic.rs:
- Replace all HashMap/HashSet fields with BTreeMap/BTreeSet in both
  JudgmentCondition and SeparationLogicVerifier. These structures are used
  in lab-mode verification where deterministic iteration order is essential
  for reproducible violation reports and snapshot comparison. Affected fields:
  holder_pending, region_pending, regions_open, regions_closed, obligations,
  closed_regions, resolved

- Fix verify_region_close to NOT mark a region as closed when preconditions
  fail. Previously, if close was called on a region with pending obligations
  (pending > 0), the violation was recorded but the region was still inserted
  into closed_regions. This meant a subsequent valid close attempt (after all
  obligations were fulfilled) would hit the "already closed" check and produce
  a second spurious violation. Now tracks precondition_ok and returns early
  before the postcondition (closed_regions.insert) when any precondition fails

- Add test verifying: first close with pending obligations produces exactly
  one quiescence violation, and second close after obligations are committed
  succeeds without a spurious "already closed" violation

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
